mnemonics: MERN Makes Every Response Nice (MongoDB, Express, React, Node)
keyPoints: MERN is a full-stack JavaScript framework consisting of MongoDB (database), Express.js (server framework), React (front-end library), and Node.js (server runtime).
It allows developers to use a single programming language (JavaScript) throughout the entire application stack.
Each component handles a specific layer: React for UI, Express/Node for server logic, and MongoDB for data storage.
commonMistakes: Confusing the roles of Express (backend framework) and React (frontend library), leading to server logic in client components.
Not properly structuring the application, which can cause tightly coupled code between the frontend and backend.
Forgetting to handle CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) when the React frontend and Express backend are running on different ports during development.
